rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical radical sulfonylation/cyclization of N-allylamides with sulfonyl hydrazides (or CF3SO2Na) to form trifluoromethylated/sulfonylated oxazolines via radical-polar crossover
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of TsNHNH2 (4a) via stepwise -3H+/-3e- to generate sulfonyl diazo radical that loses N2 to give sulfonyl radical IV; a second anodic SET oxidizes the alkyl radical adduct V to cation VI; cathode reduces protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: carbon-centered benzylic radical adduct V (after sulfonyl addition to alkene of 1a)
benzylic carbocation VI (after second anodic SET)

electron_flow: At the anode, TsNHNH2 is oxidized (-3e-, -3H+) and extrudes N2 to give a sulfonyl radical, which adds regioselectively to the alkene of the N-allylamide forming a benzylic radical; a second anodic SET oxidizes this radical to a cation that is trapped intramolecularly by the amide oxygen to give the oxazoline after deprotonation. The cathode reduces protons to H2.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: With CF3SO2Na: 3a only trace and Ph2C(OH)CF3-type adduct detected by GC-MS, indicating CF3 radical intercepted by DPE; with TsNHNH2: 5a obtained in 18% and Ts-CH=CPh2-type styrene adduct detected by GC-MS, indicating sulfonyl radical trapped by DPE

probe_reagents.name: BHT (2,6-di-tert-butyl-4-methylphenol)
probe_reagents.observation: With CF3SO2Na: 3a obtained in 23% with BHT-CF3 adduct (cyclohexadienone) detected by GC-MS; with TsNHNH2: 5a in 25% with BHT-Ts adduct detected by GC-MS, supporting CF3• and Ts• intermediates

probe_reagents.name: H-type divided cell experiment
probe_reagents.observation: Substrate + 2a/K2CO3 (or 4a) only in anodic chamber gave 3a (57%) and 5a (39%), confirming the key bond-forming events occur at the anode

notes: Two coupled systems are described: (i) CF3SO2Na (2a) with K2CO3 in MeCN/H2O giving 3a (CF3-oxazoline), and (ii) TsNHNH2 (4a) with Me4NBF4 in MeCN giving 5a (Ts-oxazoline). The scheme S3 explicitly depicts only the sulfonyl hydrazide pathway, but the CF3SO2Na variant is proposed to proceed analogously via CF3• addition and radical-polar crossover. Divided-cell experiments locate the productive chemistry at the anode; DPE and BHT trapping experiments support discrete sulfonyl/CF3 radical intermediates.
